data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The collapse was not a failure of the system but a successful, coordinated strategy of de-urbanization by subordinate kinship groups who deliberately rejected palatial authority to escape extraction.", "mechanism": "Bottom-up resistance: Subaltern groups, empowered by the diffusion of iron technology or new social ideologies, actively dismantled palace centers to reclaim autonomy, viewing the 'collapse' as liberation rather than catastrophe.", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Levantine city-states", "Hittite periphery"], "excludes": ["Egypt", "core Hittite capital Hattusa"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Mass graves or destruction layers indicating violent conquest by external Sea Peoples rather than internal abandonment or burning by inhabitants.", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Adopts the counter-hegemonic stance (artifact ff56075b), reframing the event as agency-driven de-complexification rather than systemic crash."}
